hero.title: Free Online
hero.description: Crop JPG/JPEG images with adjustable quality. Fast, free, and privacy-focused.

alerts.fileSizeLimit: File size must be less than 10MB
alerts.selectLargerArea: Please select a larger area to crop
alerts.cropFailed: Failed to crop image. Please try again.

dropzone.orClickBrowse: or click to browse a file
dropzone.maxSizeHint: Max 10MB - JPG output with quality control

howTo.title: How To Crop JPG Images Online
howTo.step1Desc: Drag any image file into the editor or click to pick one from your device. Works with JPEG, PNG, WebP, and more.
howTo.step2Desc: Pick a preset ratio or draw a freeform selection, then adjust the quality slider to control file size.
howTo.step3Desc: Hit crop, preview the result, and save your optimized JPEG in one click.
howTo.tipText: Use 80-90% quality for photos for smaller files with minimal visible difference.

why.title: Why Crop JPG Images?
why.p1: JPEG is the workhorse format for photography on the web. Cropping removes unused areas so the final file becomes lighter and faster to load.
why.p2: If you do not need transparency, JPG is usually the best choice over PNG for page speed and bandwidth savings.
why.b1: Great for product photos, blog images, and social media visuals.
why.b2: The quality slider gives direct control over visual quality versus file size.
why.b3: Lighter images improve page speed, Core Web Vitals, and user experience.

proTips.title: Pro Tips for JPG Cropping
proTips.t1: Match your crop ratio to where the image will be used, such as 16:9 for banners or 1:1 for square feeds.
proTips.t2: For most web photos, 80-85% quality gives an excellent balance between quality and size.
proTips.t3: JPEG does not support transparency. Use PNG or WebP if you need a transparent background.
proTips.t4: For print, use 95-100% quality. For email and chat previews, 70-80% is usually enough.

items.question: Will cropping my JPG reduce image quality?
items.answer: JPEG uses lossy compression, so there is a small re-encoding step. At 85-90% quality, differences are usually invisible.

items.question: What quality setting should I use for website images?
items.answer: Start around 75-85% for a strong quality-size balance. Increase only when fine details are critical.

items.question: Can I crop PNG or WebP and save as JPG?
items.answer: Yes. You can upload common image formats and export the crop as JPG. Transparent regions become a solid background.

items.question: Why is my cropped file larger than expected?
items.answer: Very high JPG quality can create larger files, especially for small crops. Lowering quality to 80-85% usually helps.

items.question: Does any file data leave my device?
items.answer: No. Cropping and JPG encoding run locally in your browser. Nothing is uploaded to a server.
